assertions.description: The indexedDB factory is exposed on Window.
IDBRequest exposes the error property used to report failed reads.
IDBTransaction exposes an error property for aborted transactions.
A failed IndexedDB request surfaces a DOMException through request.error.
assertions.test: (async()=>{if(!('indexedDB' in globalThis))return false;const name='idb-conformance-'+Date.now()+'-'+Math.random();return await new Promise(resolve=>{const open=indexedDB.open(name,1);let settled=false;const done=(ok)=>{if(settled)return;settled=true;try{if(open.result)open.result.close();}catch(_){}try{indexedDB.deleteDatabase(name);}catch(_){}resolve(ok);};open.onerror=()=>done(false);open.onupgradeneeded=()=>{open.result.createObjectStore('s',{keyPath:'id'});};open.onsuccess=()=>{const db=open.result;const tx=db.transaction('s','readwrite');const store=tx.objectStore('s');store.add({id:1});const dup=store.add({id:1});dup.onerror=()=>{done(dup.error instanceof DOMException && dup.error.name==='ConstraintError');};tx.oncomplete=()=>done(false);tx.onabort=()=>{};};setTimeout(()=>done(false),4000);});})()
